query: Shapiro for group A: p = 0.12. Shapiro for group B: p = 0.001. Levene: p = 0.04. What now?
expected_behavior: Flags Group B as non-Normal (Shapiro p = 0.001 < 0.05)
Recommends Mann-Whitney U as the primary path (or Welch's t as an explicitly-named fallback that addresses only the variance issue)
